description.en: Return the Euclidean norm, sqrt(sum(x**2 for x in coordinates)). This is the length of the vector from the origin to the point given by the coordinates. For a two dimensional point (x, y), this is equivalent to computing the hypotenuse of a right triangle using the Pythagorean theorem, sqrt(x*x + y*y). Changed in version 3.8: Added support for n-dimensional points. Formerly, only the two dimensional case was supported. Changed in version 3.10: Improved the algorithm’s accuracy so that the max...
